trigger: User visual review showed that the green allowed and amber protected geometry panel itself can be wrong, broad, conflicting, or untrustworthy even before semantic mask QA.
non_negotiable_rule: No Wave70 mask can be accepted, candidate-passed, locally passed, complete, generalized, certification-ready, target-runtime-ready, or used as canonical protected geometry unless the exact row has a passing geometry gate with the required approval token.
geometry_fail_examples: green allowed and amber protected regions overlap in a confusing or contradictory way
green allowed region is a broad rectangle that does not trace the named anatomical target
amber protected region is a broad rectangle that does not trace real protected anatomy
mask is considered valid because it sits inside a green box while missing the actual body part
full-image, crop, mask, and panel coordinate transforms are missing or unproven
panel is too small, cluttered, or low-resolution to inspect
rectangle-only or hardcoded one-image geometry is treated as proof instead of debug
current_status: Existing worked Wave70 masks remain untrusted. Generated-output stability remains output-geometry evidence only and cannot promote geometry, mask alignment, boundary correctness, or completion.
applies_to: future neck, ears, hairline, hair, hands, fingers, fingernails, skin, clothing, contact, body-part, video, audio, and deformation masks
statement: This evidence does not pass any mask. It locks in the rule that geometry correctness is a separate prerequisite gate before semantic mask alignment, protected-neighbor QA, generated-output QA, reference-matrix QA, target-runtime proof, or certification.
